business_hours: Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 7:00 PM MST. We extended by an hour because of high call volume in the evening
emergency_definition: Any situation involving flooding, fire, gas leaks, no heat in winter, no AC when temperatures exceed 95°F, security breaches, or elevator malfunctions. We lowered the AC threshold and added elevator issues
emergency_routing_rules: Transfer to on-call maintenance supervisor at extension 501. If no answer, try backup number 303-555-0199, and now also try the property manager cell at 303-555-0250 as a third option
non_emergency_routing_rules: Take a detailed message including the property address and unit number, and schedule a callback for the next business day
call_transfer_rules: During office hours, transfer to the front desk at extension 100 first. If no answer after 20 seconds, try extension 200. If still no answer, try the office manager at extension 150
integration_constraints: We're now testing an AppFolio API integration for logging maintenance requests. Clara should still handle calls normally but note that automated ticket creation is being piloted
after_hours_flow_summary: After hours: Greet caller, determine urgency, collect info, route emergencies or take messages for callbacks.
office_hours_flow_summary: Standard office hours protocol: greet caller, identify purpose, collect name and phone, transfer to appropriate party, fallback if unavailable.
questions_or_unknowns: Sarah: Any update to the emergency definition?
Sarah: Any changes to the call transfer rules?
notes: We hired a new assistant property manager, Janet, who handles tenant complaints directly. Her extension is 175. Also, we now have a Spanish-speaking staff member available at extension 160 for callers who prefer Spanish.
